qa_pairs.question: What database servers are available to researchers through the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance provides access to MySQL and Postgres database servers for researchers.

qa_pairs.question: On which clusters are the Alliance's database servers available?
qa_pairs.answer: The database servers are available on both the Cedar and Graham clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What is the status of database server availability on the Graham cluster as of January 13, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: As of January 13, 2025, no database server will be provided on Graham during the transition to the new Nibi system, as the cluster will be operating at approximately 25% capacity.

qa_pairs.question: What is the general description for the Cedar - MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: It is a general-purpose server for researchers who want to set up SQL tables and issue SQL commands against them.

qa_pairs.question: What is the general description for the Cedar - Postgres server?
qa_pairs.answer: It is a general-purpose server for researchers who want to set up SQL tables and issue SQL commands against them. It also includes a PostGIS extension for geocoding needs.

qa_pairs.question: What is the general description for the Graham - MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: It is a general-purpose server for researchers who want to set up SQL tables and issue SQL commands against them.

qa_pairs.question: What is the full server name for Cedar - MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: The full server name for Cedar - MySQL is cedar-mysql-vm.int.cedar.computecanada.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the short server name for Cedar - MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: The short server name for Cedar - MySQL is cedar-mysql-vm.

qa_pairs.question: What is the latest version of MariaDB available on Cedar - MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: The latest version available on Cedar - MySQL is MariaDB version 11.5.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find documentation for MariaDB on Cedar - MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: Documentation for MariaDB is available on the MariaDB website.

qa_pairs.question: What is the full server name for Cedar - Postgres?
qa_pairs.answer: The full server name for Cedar - Postgres is cedar-pgsql-vm.int.cedar.computecanada.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the short server name for Cedar - Postgres?
qa_pairs.answer: The short server name for Cedar - Postgres is cedar-pgsql-vm.

qa_pairs.question: What is the latest version of PostgreSQL available on Cedar - Postgres, and what PostGIS version is included?
qa_pairs.answer: Cedar - Postgres offers PostgreSQL version 16 and PostGIS version 3.3 extension.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find documentation for PostgreSQL and PostGIS on Cedar - Postgres?
qa_pairs.answer: Documentation is available on the Postgres website and the PostGIS documentation website.

qa_pairs.question: What is the full server name and IP address for Graham - MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: The full server name for Graham - MySQL is cc-gra-dbaas1.sharcnet.ca, and its IP is 199.241.163.99.

qa_pairs.question: Does Graham - MySQL have a short server name?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Graham - MySQL does not have a short server name available.

qa_pairs.question: What is the latest version of MariaDB available on Graham - MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: The latest version available on Graham - MySQL is MariaDB version 11.5.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find documentation for MariaDB on Graham - MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: Documentation for MariaDB on Graham - MySQL is available on the MariaDB website.

qa_pairs.question: What database system does the Cedar MySQL server utilize?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar MySQL server utilizes MariaDB, which is compatible with other flavours of MySQL.

qa_pairs.question: What is the VM name for the MariaDB server on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: The MariaDB server runs as a VM called "cedar-mysql-vm" with the full name cedar-mysql-vm.int.cedar.computecanada.ca.

qa_pairs.question: How can users connect to the MySQL server on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: Users with accounts on the MySQL server can connect only through the Cedar head node (cedar.computecanada.ca), Cedar compute nodes, and the Cedar Portal.

qa_pairs.question: Can I connect to the Cedar MySQL database server directly via SSH?
qa_pairs.answer: No, for security reasons, users cannot make an SSH connection directly to the database server.

qa_pairs.question: When is a MySQL account required on the Cedar MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: A MySQL account is required if you need the privileges to create your own database.

qa_pairs.question: How do I request a MySQL account on the Cedar MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to send a request to Technical Support with your Alliance username and the amount of database space needed for your project.

qa_pairs.question: What information should I include in my request for a Cedar MySQL account?
qa_pairs.answer: You should include your Alliance username and the amount of database space needed for your project.

qa_pairs.question: How will my MySQL account username be determined?
qa_pairs.answer: Your MySQL account will be created with the same username as your Alliance username.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of password is provided for a new MySQL account on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: A 16-digit random string password will be provided for your new MySQL account.

qa_pairs.question: Where is the MySQL connection information stored?
qa_pairs.answer: The username, password, database server name, and other connection information are stored in a file named `.my.cnf` in your home directory.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the `.my.cnf` file?
qa_pairs.answer: The `.my.cnf` file stores your confidential MySQL connection details, including username, password, and server name, which are automatically used by the `mysql` client.

qa_pairs.question: Can I modify the contents of the `.my.cnf` file?
qa_pairs.answer: No, you cannot change the contents of the `.my.cnf` file, but you can read or delete it.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I delete the `.my.cnf` file?
qa_pairs.answer: If the `.my.cnf` file is deleted, you will lose access to your database.

qa_pairs.question: Which client should I use to connect to the MySQL server, and how can I load the recommended version?
qa_pairs.answer: You should use the "mysql" client. It's recommended to load a more recent version by issuing `module load mariadb` followed by `mariadb --version`.

qa_pairs.question: How can I test if my MySQL database account is set up correctly?
qa_pairs.answer: You can test it by running `mysql` and then executing `show databases;` and `quit;` in the MariaDB prompt.

qa_pairs.question: What `mysql` command options should I avoid when connecting?
qa_pairs.answer: You should not use the `-p` or `-h` options as arguments when running `mysql`.

qa_pairs.question: Why should I avoid using the `-p` or `-h` options when running the `mysql` command?
qa_pairs.answer: The required password and server name are automatically taken from your `.my.cnf` file, so these options are unnecessary and should be avoided.

qa_pairs.question: Is it acceptable to run long-running SQL commands from the head node on Cedar MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is acceptable for long-running SQL commands, as most of the CPU usage is taken from the database server side.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my SQL script uses a lot of CPU?
qa_pairs.answer: If your SQL script issues commands and uses a lot of CPU, it needs to be submitted as a job to the scheduler.

qa_pairs.question: What is required to set up MySQL tables and query them?
qa_pairs.answer: To set up MySQL tables and query them, you need to create your own database.

qa_pairs.question: What is the naming convention for creating a new MySQL database?
qa_pairs.answer: The database name is arbitrary but must start with "username_" where "username" is your MySQL username.

qa_pairs.question: Can I create multiple MySQL databases on Cedar MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you may create multiple MySQL databases as long as they all start with "username_".

qa_pairs.question: How would a user named "david" create a database named "david_db1"?
qa_pairs.answer: The commands would be `mysql`, then at the MariaDB prompt: `CREATE DATABASE david_db1;` and `quit;`.

qa_pairs.question: Who has access to the databases I create on Cedar MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: The created databases will automatically be accessible only to you from the Cedar head node, Cedar compute nodes, and the Cedar Portal.

qa_pairs.question: What privileges do I have for SQL objects within my created MySQL databases?
qa_pairs.answer: You will have full privileges to create SQL objects such as tables, views, etc.
